raw_consumer_statement: I received a XXXX XXXX refund. However, it was sent to my old Best Buy Citi account that has been closed. I am sending proof of receipts showing, I was sent the price match refund for my tv. I was told a check would be mailed out with in 3-5 days. It did not happen. My XXXX XXXX refund went to my old Best Buy Citi account ending in XXXX. It should have went to the card ending in XXXX per the receipts. I had a tv go out and took out the 5 year warranty. Best Buy gave me {$1200.00} towards a new tv and l upgraded to a more expensive tv paying the difference of {$1100.00} as shown in the receipts. Again, the {$600.00} XXXX XXXX refund was suppose to go to card ending in XXXX original payment method for that {$1100.00} difference I paid from for the difference. This has been going on for MONTHS with no resolution to this day. Citi says the account was credited on the old account that was closed and I had a balance. Not so, I filed that account in a bankruptcy and was XXXX a few years ago. Citi has no business keeping this account active and not fully closed. Apparently, this scenario has happened to many other customers. Best Buy themselves see this happen frequently. This is very wrong for Citi to continue these practices and get away with it. My local store has been working with me trying to get this resolved. They can not get any where with it. Initially, Best Buy said to contact Citi. I did and opened a dispute. Only for them to send me a letter saying to take it up with Best Buy directly. They are passing it back and fourth leaving me in the middle with no resolution to this day. Citi even acknowledged themselves yes, they received the credit and posted it to that old/closed account ending in XXXX. That is not their money to keep and posted it on the wrong account. My next step is small claims court. It's a slam dunk case and I have all the documents to prove it. Even more documents if requested or needed. I don't want to go that route as this can be easily addressed. Citi is just being difficult for what ever reason.
